verses.text: God invites Noah: Come, enter into the ark. Notice the language — God doesn't drive him in, He welcomes him. He also specifies seven pairs of clean animals — for later sacrifice and food — and one pair of unclean.

God invites Noah: Come, enter into the ark. Notice the language — God doesn't drive him in, He welcomes him. He also specifies seven pairs of clean animals — for later sacrifice and food — and one pair of unclean.

God invites Noah: Come, enter into the ark. Notice the language — God doesn't drive him in, He welcomes him. He also specifies seven pairs of clean animals — for later sacrifice and food — and one pair of unclean.
After seven days, the rain will come — forty days and forty nights. Seven days of waiting. Genesis often gives time to enter, to repent, before judgment falls.
And Noah did according unto all that the LORD commanded him. The pattern continues — perfect obedience.
Noah was 600 years old when the flood came. The chapter slows down to mark the exact moment in his life.
Noah, his wife, his three sons, and their wives enter the ark — and the animals come, two by two, as God commanded. The procession is orderly and complete.
Noah, his wife, his three sons, and their wives enter the ark — and the animals come, two by two, as God commanded. The procession is orderly and complete.
Noah, his wife, his three sons, and their wives enter the ark — and the animals come, two by two, as God commanded. The procession is orderly and complete.

After seven days the waters came. The fountains of the great deep burst open, and the windows of heaven were opened. This is creation in reverse — the waters above and below, which God separated on day two, rush back in.

After seven days the waters came. The fountains of the great deep burst open, and the windows of heaven were opened. This is creation in reverse — the waters above and below, which God separated on day two, rush back in.

After seven days the waters came. The fountains of the great deep burst open, and the windows of heaven were opened. This is creation in reverse — the waters above and below, which God separated on day two, rush back in.
The text re-tells the boarding, this time listing every name and species — Noah, Shem, Ham, Japheth, their wives, every beast after its kind. Genesis is making sure no one is forgotten.
The text re-tells the boarding, this time listing every name and species — Noah, Shem, Ham, Japheth, their wives, every beast after its kind. Genesis is making sure no one is forgotten.
The text re-tells the boarding, this time listing every name and species — Noah, Shem, Ham, Japheth, their wives, every beast after its kind. Genesis is making sure no one is forgotten.
Every creature came in, two by two, as God commanded — and the LORD shut him in. Noah didn't close the door. God did.

The waters rose for forty days. The ark floated upward, lifted above the earth. Even the highest mountains were covered by more than fifteen cubits — about twenty feet — of water. There is nothing left to climb to.

The waters rose for forty days. The ark floated upward, lifted above the earth. Even the highest mountains were covered by more than fifteen cubits — about twenty feet — of water. There is nothing left to climb to.

The waters rose for forty days. The ark floated upward, lifted above the earth. Even the highest mountains were covered by more than fifteen cubits — about twenty feet — of water. There is nothing left to climb to.

The waters rose for forty days. The ark floated upward, lifted above the earth. Even the highest mountains were covered by more than fifteen cubits — about twenty feet — of water. There is nothing left to climb to.

All flesh died — birds, cattle, beasts, creeping things, and humans. The text repeats the destruction four times. Only Noah and those with him in the ark remained alive. God's promise from chapter six is being kept.

All flesh died — birds, cattle, beasts, creeping things, and humans. The text repeats the destruction four times. Only Noah and those with him in the ark remained alive. God's promise from chapter six is being kept.
The waters prevailed upon the earth a hundred and fifty days — about five months. The world waits, submerged.
